sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) part, portion (from the root מָנַן). The proper force of this word as a noun does not occur in the common use of the O. T. (for the idea is unsuitable as proposed by Jo. Simonis, who takes מִנֵּהוּ Psa 68:24 as his or their portion, i.e. of the dogs); but it is manifest however in the forms מִמֶּנִּי pr. a parte mea, de ma part, on my part, my behalf (comp. Eze 3:17, “warn them מִמֶּנִּי” Germ. don meinetwegen, from me), מִמֶּנּוּ a parte ejus, and the prefix מִן pr. is its const. state. That this is really a subst. the pl. const. מִנֵּי ( Isa 30:11) so manifests as to leave no room for doubting.

(2) pl. מִנִּים strings of a musical instrument, pr. slender threads, so called from being divided. Psa 150:4. Syr. ܡܶܢܝܺܢ id. Prob. we should also here refer, Psa 45:9, “out of the ivory palaces מִנִּי שִׂמְּחוּךָ the strings (i.e. concerts of music) gladden thee.” As to the plur. termination ־ִי for ־ִים (which some have of late been too desirous to exclude from grammars), see Lehrg. p. 525, 526.
